name: Prompt Engineering Effects Search Pass

search_queries.result_count: screened first result page for targeted query
screened first result page for targeted query
screened first result page for targeted query

search_queries.query: "synthetic student inputs" Copilot prompt high quality low quality typo
search_queries.result_count: screened first result page for targeted query

search_queries.query: "Calibrating Generative AI to Produce Realistic Essays" prompt strategies
search_queries.result_count: reviewed arXiv abstract, HTML/search snippets, and PDF link

inclusion_criteria: The source compares prompt strategies or reports prompt ingredients that affect generated synthetic student responses or synthetic student inputs.
The source reports prompt-conditioned generation for student response, proxy-student, item-evaluation, or chatbot-input contexts.
Direct findings for the baseline require accessible full text; abstract-only or access-limited sources may be retained only as screened/deferred candidates.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude sources whose primary variation is model family, post-training, history conditioning, or workflow design without prompt ingredients as a central variable.
Exclude generic LLM tutoring or feedback studies that do not generate or evaluate synthetic student responses or inputs.
Exclude abstract-only or paywalled sources from direct synthesis support until full text is obtained or the finding is explicitly limited to abstract-level discovery.
dedupe_method: Deduplicated by title, DOI, arXiv identifier, venue metadata, and canonical source page.

screening_counts.records_identified: at least 25 web and primary-source records across targeted queries

screening_decisions.title: Calibrating Generative AI to Produce Realistic Essays for Data Augmentation
screening_decisions.reason: Directly compares 25 Examples, Predict Next, and Sentence prompt strategies for simulated essays and reports score agreement, weighted kappa, average score, and realism detection results.

screening_decisions.title: Can LLMs Reliably Simulate Real Students' Abilities in Mathematics and Reading Comprehension?
screening_decisions.reason: Directly compares unenforced and grade-enforced prompt families for proxy-student NAEP item answering and reports prompt-specific IRT alignment changes.

screening_decisions.title: Generative Students: Using LLM-Simulated Student Profiles to Support Question Item Evaluation
screening_decisions.reason: Reports prompt-architecture choices for GPT-4 simulated profiles, examples, instructor role prediction, and one-question-at-a-time prompting, with profile-rule response alignment results.

screening_decisions.title: A Framework for Designing an AI Chatbot to Support Scientific Argumentation
screening_decisions.reason: Provides prompt-conditioned synthetic student inputs generated from high-quality, low-quality, and typo-conditioned examples for chatbot prompt testing; useful as boundary evidence for prompt-conditioned stress-test input generation.

screening_decisions.title: Generating Student Responses Based on Knowledge Component Mastery Profiles
screening_decisions.reason: Potentially relevant to prompt/profile-conditioned response generation, but this pass did not verify full-text access and does not use it as direct support.

screening_decisions.title: Using ChatGPT to generate and score open-ended assessment tasks
screening_decisions.reason: Potentially relevant to prompt-guided generated responses and scoring, but current source metadata points to a ResearchGate page and the pass did not verify primary full text.

screening_decisions.title: Agent4Edu: Generating Learner Response Data by Generative Agents for Intelligent Education Systems
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant source with full-text access, but the already-published evidence is stronger for agent workflow and model-generation effects than prompt-engineering effects alone.

screening_decisions.title: Simulated Students in Tutoring Dialogues: Substance or Illusion?
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant source with full-text access, but the strongest contrast is prompting versus SFT/DPO post-training, already covered under model and generation effects.

screening_decisions.title: Leveraging LLM Respondents for Item Evaluation: A Psychometric Analysis
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant source with full-text access, but the staged evidence is primarily model and ensemble sensitivity rather than prompt design.

screening_decisions.title: DRESS: Dataset for Rubric-based Essay Scoring on ELL Writings
screening_decisions.reason: Useful for synthetic/corrupted writing and rubric scoring context, but not a prompt-engineering source for synthetic student response generation in this pass.

screening_summary: Targeted bootstrap screening included four prompt-focused sources: a direct simulated-essay prompt-strategy comparison, a direct grade-enforced proxy-student prompt comparison, a profile/example/role prompt-architecture source for MCQ item evaluation, and a boundary source on prompt-conditioned synthetic chatbot inputs. Access-limited or scope-adjacent sources were not used for direct synthesis support.
limitations: This was a targeted bootstrap pass, not a systematic review.
Prompt ingredients are not isolated uniformly across included sources; some evidence is prompt-family or prompt-architecture package evidence.
Included studies span essays, NAEP multiple-choice ability simulation, psychology MCQ item evaluation, and scientific-argumentation chatbot inputs.
The pass prioritized accessible primary full text. Access-limited candidates should be revisited before any direct finding is created from them.
